meaning: n.火球,大流星,[军](旧式)燃烧弹,[美口]工作起来劲十足的人
examples: Fire groups were fighting the fire on Sunday when a sudden change of wind direction caused "a huge fireball".
I hope John will join us. He is a fireball.
The fireball appears on the right side of Wesley's image.
You know that one spark and your car would go up in a hyperinflationary fireball.
The American League champion Red Sox employ a more traditional closer in young fireball Jonathan Papelbon.
